title: Planning State Machine Specification
purpose: Design the planning orchestration layer that bridges ambiguous human intent to deterministic, immutable execution contracts through a formal state machine.
architectural_constraints: Planning and execution MUST be separate layers
Compiled plans are immutable execution contracts once approved
Ambiguity must be resolved before compilation, never during execution
JSON is authority; Markdown is projection
Plans must not directly reference branches — abstraction: Plan → Milestone → Ticket → Branch

deliverables.description: Architecture Decision Record documenting planning state machine design
Schema for raw feature intake records
Protocol defining intake → ambiguity resolution → compilation lifecycle
Protocol for compiling stabilized specifications into executable plans
Protocol for structured ambiguity detection and clarification loops

acceptance_criteria: ADR documents complete state machine: DRAFT → RESOLVING → STABILIZED → COMPILED → APPROVED → SCHEDULED → ACTIVE → COMPLETE → ARCHIVED
All schemas validate against JSON Schema draft-07
Protocols define clear entry/exit conditions for each state transition
Ambiguity resolution protocol includes ambiguity_score and operator_override mechanisms
Plan compilation produces immutable execution contracts with ticket bindings
Execution authorization protocol integrates with plan state machine
Reviewed and approved before implementation begins

traceability.description: Created planning state machine schemas and protocols per ADR-003
